summary: Cross-chain address and asset mapping for the pGALA incident. The legacy peg (pGALA <-> ETH GALA) was the trust anchor that broke when the BSC custodian retained its old signer.

ethereum.note: Original GALA token on Ethereum (pre-2022-05 contract). The deprecated v1 token; v2 was deployed in May 2022 but the bridge accounting was not migrated until after the incident.

bsc_relaunch.note: Re-issued by pNetwork after the incident; out of fuzzer scope.

user_identity.ethereum_address: <not used in this incident — attacker only acted on BSC>
user_identity.note: The exploit only required a BSC-side signature using a stale ECDSA key. No Ethereum-side action was performed by the attacker.
